subject: Fix policy api credentials for oof in oom
commit_message:

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

waiting for @krzysztof review

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Temporary -1. We need to resolve username/password with policy team as healthcheck is not a suitable username

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> Temporary -1. We need to resolve username/password with policy team as healthcheck is not a suitable username

Well, this is not a critical issue. And at this stage such change is problematic. This password is used in many demos. Change may result with necessity of additional development and test of all the demos that use policy in the last moment...

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

@krzysztof, are you OK with Lukasz or do you want a login/pwd change?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: -Code-Review
> 
> @krzysztof, are you OK with Lukasz or do you want a login/pwd change?

I'd prefer to change this as using healthcheck credentials for real usage is terrible practice. I'm just waiting for merge of our password removal changes to avoid conflicts

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1: -Code-Review
> > 
> > @krzysztof, are you OK with Lukasz or do you want a login/pwd change?
> 
> I'd prefer to change this as using healthcheck credentials for real usage is terrible practice. I'm just waiting for merge of our password removal changes to avoid conflicts

Password removal changes at this stage? How does it comply with use case test status where most of them are already completed.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 1: -Code-Review
> > > 
> > > @krzysztof, are you OK with Lukasz or do you want a login/pwd change?
> > 
> > I'd prefer to change this as using healthcheck credentials for real usage is terrible practice. I'm just waiting for merge of our password removal changes to avoid conflicts
> 
> Password removal changes at this stage? How does it comply with use case test status where most of them are already completed.

Those changes are in review for some time now (way before we branched out frankfurt). They don't modify any container images just the helm charts. Also those changes are transparent for the application as we run init container to make substitution in application config files. Password won't be also auto-generated it will be the same hardcoded password just passed as a secret instead of configmap

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 1:
> > > 
> > > > Patch Set 1: -Code-Review
> > > > 
> > > > @krzysztof, are you OK with Lukasz or do you want a login/pwd change?
> > > 
> > > I'd prefer to change this as using healthcheck credentials for real usage is terrible practice. I'm just waiting for merge of our password removal changes to avoid conflicts
> > 
> > Password removal changes at this stage? How does it comply with use case test status where most of them are already completed.
> 
> Those changes are in review for some time now (way before we branched out frankfurt). They don't modify any container images just the helm charts. Also those changes are transparent for the application as we run init container to make substitution in application config files. Password won't be also auto-generated it will be the same hardcoded password just passed as a secret instead of configmap

We have another password issue, which needs to be updated: https://jira.onap.org/browse/OPTFRA-756
